attributes.description: The name of the checkbox, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
attributes.name: name

attributes.description: The current value of the checkbox, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
attributes.name: value

attributes.description: Sets the checkbox to a readonly state.
attributes.name: readonly

attributes.description: Draws the checkbox in a checked state.
attributes.name: checked

attributes.description:
Draws the checkbox in an indeterminate state. This is usually applied to checkboxes that represents a "select
all/none" behavior when associated checkboxes have a mix of checked and unchecked states.
attributes.name: indeterminate

attributes.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.
attributes.name: form

attributes.description: Makes the checkbox a required field.
attributes.name: required

attributes.description: The checkbox's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.
attributes.name: help-text

cssParts.description: The square container that wraps the checkbox's checked state.
cssParts.name: control

cssParts.description: Matches the control part when the checkbox is checked.
cssParts.name: control--checked

cssParts.description: Matches the control part when the checkbox is indeterminate.
cssParts.name: control--indeterminate

cssParts.description: The container that wraps the checkbox's label.
cssParts.name: label

events.description: Emitted when the checkbox loses focus.
events.name: syn-blur

events.description: Emitted when the checked state changes.
events.name: syn-change

events.description: Emitted when the checkbox gains focus.
events.name: syn-focus

events.description: Emitted when the checkbox receives input.
events.name: syn-input

events.description: Emitted when the form control has been checked for validity and its constraints aren't satisfied.
events.name: syn-invalid

methods.description: Simulates a click on the checkbox.
methods.name: click

methods.description: Checks for validity but does not show a validation message. Returns `true` when valid and `false` when invalid.
methods.name: checkValidity

methods.description: Gets the associated form, if one exists.
methods.name: getForm

methods.description: Checks for validity and shows the browser's validation message if the control is invalid.
methods.name: reportValidity

methods.description:
Sets a custom validation message. The value provided will be shown to the user when the form is submitted. To clear
the custom validation message, call this method with an empty string.
methods.name: setCustomValidity

properties.description: The name of the checkbox, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
properties.name: name

properties.description: The current value of the checkbox, submitted as a name/value pair with form data.
properties.name: value

properties.description: Sets the checkbox to a readonly state.
properties.name: readonly

properties.description: Draws the checkbox in a checked state.
properties.name: checked

properties.description:
Draws the checkbox in an indeterminate state. This is usually applied to checkboxes that represents a "select
all/none" behavior when associated checkboxes have a mix of checked and unchecked states.
properties.name: indeterminate

properties.description: The default value of the form control. Primarily used for resetting the form control.
properties.name: defaultChecked

properties.description:
By default, form controls are associated with the nearest containing `<form>` element. This attribute allows you
to place the form control outside of a form and associate it with the form that has this `id`. The form must be in
the same document or shadow root for this to work.
properties.name: form

properties.description: Makes the checkbox a required field.
properties.name: required

properties.description: The checkbox's help text. If you need to display HTML, use the `help-text` slot instead.
properties.name: helpText

slots.description: Text that describes how to use the checkbox. Alternatively, you can use the `help-text` attribute.
slots.name: help-text

summary: Checkboxes allow the user to toggle an option on or off.
